title: A Question About the Tree of Life
display_title: A Question About the Tree of Life
wikitext:
[]: A Question About the Tree of Life             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Mon Jan 28, 2008 3:40 am I apologize if I'm spamming up the forum with a question that's already been asked, but as I'm watching EoE again right now to pass the time, I got a bit confused at Fuyutsuki's quote. When the Spear of Longinus penetrated Unit-01's core, he said the Tree of Life was formed again . So .. I was confused as to when the Tree of Life was formed. . . you know, the first time . o_o I thought the Tree of Life only formed as a union of Adam and Lilith, which is the only reason it could possibly form (right? XD). But, with all the reading I've done on Adam and Lilith lately, everything points to the fact that they've never united in the past, simply because Adam became inactive (or does the Lilin DNA "diving" into Adam at Second Impact qualify as a "union" in the same sense?). Perhaps I'm just ignorant of something that was mentioned before, but since I really want to know what he meant (and didn't have time to search through 30+ pages), I'd appreciate it if someone could shed some light on this (or at least nudge me towards another thread XD).

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 28, 2008 3:52 am If I remember correctly (which I usually don't) the Tree of life is the source of all life (?). It's got the Fruit of Knowledge (FoK) and the Fruit of Life (FoL). And blah, blah, blah. Something about Lilith. Blah. I don't know.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 28, 2008 4:14 am The Tree of Life hasn't been formed again in exactly that sense. From what little I know, the language indicates that Eva-01 is being "returned" or "restored" to some more primitive state, which might go along with the "embryo of a life" bit (as in, "Eva-01 is being restored to the Tree of Life, the embryo of a life"). It's really quite obtuse and makes no obvious sense, though there's speculation floating around, and various old ANF threads addressing the area that, if not archived already, can be dug up.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jan 28, 2008 7:57 am Lance pierces Sho, ToL is formed, Fuyu says something about "embryo of lives", interpretation from mass ambiguity ensues. My guess is that it has something to do with Sho becoming a Seed of Life - like Adam and Lilith did back wherever they came from.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 28, 2008 12:34 pm It's not necessarily "lives" plural. The embryo bit could be in reference to no more than the "return to an earlier state", which fits contextually. The question would thus be why the Tree of Life is considered some kind of "return" or "restoration".

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jan 28, 2008 1:11 pm Reichu wrote: The question would thus be why the Tree of Life is considered some kind of "return" or "restoration".

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Jan 28, 2008 1:16 pm It's NGE, NO ONE KNOWS !!!!! Had to do it...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 28, 2008 1:22 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Return? Well, if we think of the Evas as unnatural spawns of Adam/Lilith, then perhaps that's what the return is speaking of?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jan 28, 2008 1:30 pm Well... Origins, think about it: TOL -> SOL (Adam/Lilith) -> Evas So, by an Eva becoming the ToL, it's a return to origins. Yes, that sounds alright.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 28, 2008 1:58 pm Your logic is still as slippery as an eel, Mr. Jimbo.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Jan 28, 2008 2:00 pm AsukaChan wrote: I apologize if I'm spamming up the forum with a question that's already been asked, but as I'm watching EoE again right now to pass the time, I got a bit confused at Fuyutsuki's quote. When the Spear of Longinus penetrated Unit-01's core, he said the Tree of Life was formed again . So .. I was confused as to when the Tree of Life was formed. . . you know, the first time . o_o

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Jan 28, 2008 2:11 pm Reichu wrote: transmogrification

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 28, 2008 2:28 pm Zugzwang wrote: nothing much seems to be said about what the Fruit of Knowledge is... Someone explain it to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Jan 28, 2008 11:15 pm Reichu wrote: Your logic is still as slippery as an eel, Mr. Jimbo.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 28, 2008 11:46 pm It doesn't really work if I (dunno about anyone else) still have no firm grasp upon what you're talking about, is what I'm trying to say. Also, what are you giving the thumbs up? Yourself? Whatever the case, your post has this kind of demeaning and dismissive quality to it. "You don't understand my theory, but I don't care. It's the best there is unless you can surpass it. "

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Jan 29, 2008 12:27 pm Reichu wrote: It doesn't really work if I (dunno about anyone else) still have no firm grasp upon what you're talking about, is what I'm trying to say.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Tue Jan 29, 2008 8:12 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: It's a somewhat widely held theory (enough qualifiers there to, uhhh, qualify... hopefully) that post TOL, Sho becomes a SOL herself.

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Tue Jan 29, 2008 9:12 pm I actually agree with EvaYojimbo. Unfortunately, “excessive” fanwanking on this forum will get you lynched (legs broken too).

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Jan 30, 2008 3:32 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: No it's not.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 30, 2008 7:12 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: You should talk to Dr. Nick about it, he really hates this idea because <snip>
